note: sage-attention not installing on Pod; v0.9.0 batch.

summary: Attached v0.9.0 batch plan (bake sage MPI-145 + per-card VRAM MPI-146). MPI-139 split to v0.10.0.

note: Begin sage bake (v0.10.0 batch: sage+VRAM+MPI-152 wrapper).
Sage baked per-arch (Dockerfile cu124 8.6;8.9 / cu128 12.0) + start.sh probe replaces runtime compile. Self-verified (bash -n, probe 6/6, release:check). Pending v0.10.0 build + live Pod (user-ux).

v0.10.0 build: cpu+cu128 OK (cu128 local, sage compiled+imports). cu124 CI FAILED — sage compile artifacts overflow the GitHub runner disk (prior cu124 CI builds w/o sage succeeded → sage is the delta). Fix: build cu124 LOCALLY like cu128 (no runner disk limit). cpu smoke PASS (/health wrapper_version 0.2.16). GOTCHA: baked sage tips cu124 over CI runner disk → cu124 must build local.

LIVE cu124/4090: sage baked+imports+enabled OK (--use-sage-attention, vram --lowvram 23GiB, wrapper 0.2.16 — all confirmed in boot log). BUT gen CRASHED mid-forward: CUDA error: unspecified launch failure in comfy/ldm/lightricks/model.py:477 + embeddings_connector.py:93 (Fatal Python error: Aborted). NOT memory — was a 2s low-res gen (~12GB-card workload); lowvram patches:1112, 232MB buffer. sage is the only v0.10.0 delta injecting kernels into the LTX forward → sage-attention CUDA kernel incompatible with LTX-2.3 DiT on Ada sm_89. App showed generic OOM/disconnect dialog (MISLEADING — real cause = CUDA launch failure, WS ConnectionClosedError is just the symptom). NEXT: test sage on cu128/5090 (Blackwell 12.0) — if OK, gate sage off cu124 only; if also crashes, gate off both. Decision-2 path from the plan, but Ada not Blackwell.

SAGE WORKS ON BLACKWELL. 5090/cu128 (sage ON, OLD v0.10.0 img) FULLY COMPLETED: stage2 7/7 + upscale 3/3 + VideoVAE decode -> Prompt executed in 286.01s -> LTX_00015_.mp4 produced. So sage CRASHES on Ada sm_89 (unspecified launch failure) but RUNS CLEAN on Blackwell sm_120. VERDICT: gate sage off cu124/sm_89 ONLY, KEEP on cu128/sm_120. (286s slow = lowvram-forced RAM thrash from the 31<32 threshold bug + CPU encoder; normalvram fix will speed it.) My start.sh edit currently gates BOTH (sm_89 sm_120) — change SAGE_DISABLED_ARCHS default to just sm_89. The CUBRIC_SAGE_DISABLED_ARCHS env override lets us flip per-arch without rebuild.

summary: Merged MPI-146 (per-card VRAM mode) scope into this card - same v0.10.0 image build, co-ships + co-verifies. MPI-146 archived as merged.

DONE (card was STALE, verified against mpi-ci start.sh on disk 2026-06-28). SAGE: baked per-arch, gated sm_89-OFF (Ada LTX CUDA crash) / Blackwell sm_120-ON (5090 gen live-verified clean); SAGE_DISABLED_ARCHS defaults sm_89, R2-overridable no-rebuild. PER-CARD VRAM (merged 146): REFRAMED to aimdo-managed during MPI-156 - on v0.26+torch2.8 aimdo, --normalvram was REMOVED from ComfyUI (crashes) and --lowvram is a no-op, so the per-card threshold split no longer applies; start.sh passes NO vram flag (aimdo manages), CUBRIC_VRAM_MODE override kept for ad-hoc tests. VRAM flags tested-to-death live (MPI-156) = don't help, aimdo is load-bearing, DO NOT re-try (baked into start.sh comments). All ships via R2 publish-runtime.sh, no image rebuild. Closing.
